sections.label: Section 676
sections.original: ὤμοι ἐμῶν δεινῶν , λουτρῶν καὶ κρηνῶν ,
sections.translation: Alas for my terrible fate , the baths and springs , where the goddesses brightened the beauty from which the judgment came .

sections.label: Section 679
sections.original: τὰ δʼ ἐς κρίσιν σοι τῶνδʼ ἔθηχʼ Ἥρα κακῶν — ;
sections.translation: Regarding the judgment , Hera made it a cause of these troubles for you ?

sections.label: Section 680
sections.original: Πάριν ὡς ἀφέλοιτο —
sections.translation: To take me away from Paris —

sections.label: Section 680b
sections.original: πῶς ; αὔδα .
sections.translation: How ? Tell me .

sections.label: Section 681
sections.original: Κύπρις ᾧ μʼ ἐπένευσεν —
sections.translation: To whom Kypris had promised me .

sections.label: Section 681b
sections.original: ὦ τλᾶμον .
sections.translation: O unhappy one !

sections.label: Section 682
sections.original: τλάμων , τλάμων · ὧδʼ ἐπέλασʼ Αἰγύπτῳ .
sections.translation: Unhappy , unhappy ; and so she brought me to Egypt .

sections.label: Section 683
sections.original: εἶτʼ ἀντέδωκʼ εἴδωλον , ὡς σέθεν κλύω .
sections.translation: Then she gave him a phantom instead , as I hear from you .

sections.label: Section 684
sections.original: τὰ δὲ σὰ κατὰ μέλαθρα πάθεα πάθεα , μᾶ-
sections.translation: Sorrow , sorrow to your house ,

sections.label: Section 685
sections.original: τερ , οἲ ʼγώ .
sections.translation: mother , alas .

sections.label: Section 685b
sections.original: τί φῄς ;
sections.translation: What do you mean ?

sections.label: Section 686
sections.original: οὐκ ἔστι μάτηρ · ἀγχόνιον δὲ βρόχον
sections.translation: My mother is no more ; through shame of my disgraceful marriage she tied a noose around her neck .

sections.label: Section 688
sections.original: ὤμοι · θυγατρὸς δʼ Ἑρμιόνης ἔστιν βίος ;
sections.translation: Alas ! Is our daughter Hermione alive ?

sections.label: Section 689
sections.original: ἄγαμος ἄτεκνος , ὦ πόσι , καταστένει
sections.translation: Ah , my husband ! Unmarried , and without children , she mourns my

sections.label: Section 690
sections.original: γάμον ἄγαμον ἐμόν .
sections.translation: fatal marriage .

sections.label: Section 691
sections.original: ὦ πᾶν κατʼ ἄκρας δῶμʼ ἐμὸν πέρσας Πάρις ,
sections.translation: O Paris , who utterly destroyed my whole house , these things ruined you also , and countless bronze-clad Danaans .

sections.label: Section 694
sections.original: ἐμὲ δὲ πατρίδος ἄπο κακόποτμον ἀραίαν
sections.translation: The god cast me out , ill-fated and accursed , from my country ,

sections.label: Section 695
sections.original: ἔβαλε θεὸς ἀπό τε πόλεος ἀπό τε σέθεν ,
sections.translation: from my city , and from you , when I left my home and bed — yet I did not leave them — for a shameful marriage .

sections.label: Section 698
sections.original: εἰ καὶ τὰ λοιπὰ τῆς τύχης εὐδαίμονος
sections.translation: If indeed you should find happiness in the future , it would be a match for the past .

sections.label: Section 700
sections.original: Μενέλαε , κἀμοὶ πρόσδοτον τῆς ἡδονῆς ,
sections.translation: Menelaos , give me as well a share of that joy which I understand , but not clearly .

sections.label: Section 702
sections.original: ἀλλʼ , ὦ γεραιέ , καὶ σὺ κοινώνει λόγων .
sections.translation: Come and take part in our talk , old man , you too .

sections.label: Section 703
sections.original: οὐχ ἥδε μόχθων τῶν ἐν Ἰλίῳ βραβεύς ;
sections.translation: This woman is not the arbitrator of all the trouble in Troy ?

sections.label: Section 704
sections.original: οὐχ ἥδε , πρὸς θεῶν δʼ ἦμεν ἠπατημένοι ,
sections.translation: She is not ; I was tricked by the gods

sections.label: Section 705
sections.original: νεφέλης ἄγαλμʼ ἔχοντες ἐν χεροῖν λυγρόν .
sections.translation: and had in my arms the baneful image of a cloud .

sections.label: Section 706
sections.original: τί φῄς ;
sections.translation: What are you saying ? We suffered in vain for the sake of a cloud ?

sections.label: Section 708
sections.original: Ἥρας τάδʼ ἔργα καὶ θεῶν τρισσῶν ἔρις .
sections.translation: It was the work of Hera , and the rivalry of the three goddesses .

sections.label: Section 709
sections.original: ἡ δʼ οὖσʼ ἀληθῶς ἐστιν ἥδε σὴ δάμαρ ;
sections.translation: And the one who is truly your wife is this woman here ?

sections.label: Section 710
sections.original: αὕτη · λόγοις δʼ ἐμοῖσι πίστευσον τάδε .
sections.translation: This is she ; trust my word for that .

sections.label: Section 711
sections.original: ὦ θύγατερ , ὁ θεὸς ὡς ἔφυ τι ποικίλον
sections.translation: O daughter , how intricate and hard to trace out is the nature of the god ! In some way that is good , he twists everything about , now up , now down ; one man suffers , and one who has not suffered comes afterwards to a bad end ,

sections.label: Section 715
sections.original: βέβαιον οὐδὲν τῆς ἀεὶ τύχης ἔχων .
sections.translation: having no security in his current fortune . You and your husband have had your share of trouble , you in repute , he in the heat of battle . In his eagerness , while he was eager , he got nothing ; but now that he has achieved the greatest good fortune , he has it without cause .

sections.label: Section 720
sections.original: οὐκ ἄρα γέροντα πατέρα καὶ Διοσκόρω
sections.translation: You did not , after all , bring shame upon your old father or on the twin sons of Zeus , nor did you do such things as were spoken of . Now again I renew your wedding rites and remember the blazing torch I bore , running beside the four yoked horses ; and you ,

sections.label: Section 725
sections.original: ξὺν τῷδε νύμφη δῶμʼ ἔλειπες ὄλβιον .
sections.translation: in the chariot as a bride , were leaving your happy home with this man here . Whoever pays no reverence to his master ’ s affairs , rejoicing with him and grieving with his troubles , is worthless . Although I was born a servant , let me still be numbered among honest

sections.label: Section 730
sections.original: δούλοισι , τοὔνομʼ οὐκ ἔχων ἐλεύθερον ,
sections.translation: slaves ; my mind is free , if not my name . For this is better than to suffer double misery as one man : to have a worthless heart and , being a slave , to owe obedience to any other .

sections.label: Section 734
sections.original: ἄγʼ , ὦ γεραιέ , πολλὰ μὲν παρʼ ἀσπίδα
sections.translation: Come , old man — often by my shield

sections.label: Section 735
sections.original: μοχθήματʼ ἐξέπλησας ἐκπονῶν ἐμοί ,
sections.translation: you have had your full share of trouble and hard work — now also have a share in my success , and go tell the friends I left behind the state of matters here , as you found them , and how my fortune stands ; and bid them wait at the beach and watch eagerly for the

sections.label: Section 740
sections.original: ἀγῶνας οἳ μένουσί μʼ , ὡς ἐλπίζομεν ,
sections.translation: struggle which I expect awaits me ; and if we should be able somehow to steal this woman away from the land , tell them to keep good watch so that we may share the luck and escape , if we can , from the barbarians .

sections.label: Section 744
sections.original: ἔσται τάδʼ , ὦναξ . ἀλλά τοι τὰ μάντεων
sections.translation: It shall be done , lord . Now indeed I see how worthless

sections.label: Section 745
sections.original: ἐσεῖδον ὡς φαῦλʼ ἐστὶ καὶ ψευδῶν πλέα .
sections.translation: the seers ’ doings are , and how full of falsehood ; there was no health in the blaze of sacrifice after all , or in the cry of winged birds ; even to think that birds can help mankind is certainly foolish . For Calchas gave no word or sign to the army ,

sections.label: Section 750
sections.original: νεφέλης ὑπερθνῄσκοντας εἰσορῶν φίλους
sections.translation: when he saw his friends dying on behalf of a cloud , nor did Helenos ; but the city was taken by storm in vain . You might say : because the god did not want them to ? Then why do we consult prophets ? We ought to sacrifice to the gods and ask a blessing , but leave divination alone ;

sections.label: Section 755
sections.original: βίου γὰρ ἄλλως δέλεαρ ηὑρέθη τόδε ,
sections.translation: for this was invented otherwise , as a bait for a livelihood , and no man grows rich by sacrifices if he is idle . But sound judgment and discernment are the best of seers . Exit Messenger .

sections.label: Section 758
sections.original: ἐς ταὐτὸ κἀμοὶ δόξα μαντειῶν πέρι
sections.translation: My views about seers coincide exactly with this old man ’ s ; whoever has the gods as friends

sections.label: Section 760
sections.original: φίλους ἀρίστην μαντικὴν ἔχοι δόμοις .
sections.translation: would have the best prophecy at home .

sections.label: Section 761
sections.original: εἶἑν · τὰ μὲν δὴ δεῦρʼ ἀεὶ καλῶς ἔχει .
sections.translation: All right ; so far all is well . But how you were saved , my poor husband , from Troy , there is no gain in knowing , yet friends have a desire to learn what their friends have suffered .

sections.label: Section 765
sections.original: ἦ πόλλʼ ἀνήρου μʼ ἑνὶ λόγῳ μιᾷ θʼ ὁδῷ .
sections.translation: Truly you have asked a great deal all at once . Why should I tell you about our losses in the Aegean , and Nauplios ’ beacons on Euboia , and my visits to Crete and the cities of Libya , and the mountain-peaks of Perseus ? For I would not satisfy you with the tale ,

sections.label: Section 770
sections.original: μύθων , λέγων τʼ ἄν σοι κάκʼ ἀλγοίην ἔτι ,
sections.translation: and by telling you these evils I would suffer still , as I did when I experienced them ; and so my grief would be doubled .

sections.label: Section 772
sections.original: κάλλιον εἶπας ἤ σʼ ἀνηρόμην ἐγώ .
sections.translation: Your answer is better than my question . Leave out the rest , and tell me only this : how long were you a weary wanderer over the surface of the sea ?

sections.label: Section 775
sections.original: ἐν ναυσὶν ὢν πρὸς τοῖσιν ἐν Τροίᾳ δέκα
sections.translation: Besides those ten years in Troy , I went through seven cycles of years on board ship .

sections.label: Section 777
sections.original: φεῦ φεῦ · μακρόν γʼ ἔλεξας , ὦ τάλας , χρόνον
sections.translation: Alas , poor man , you have spoken of a long time ; and , saved from there , you have come here to the slaughter .

sections.label: Section 779
sections.original: πῶς φῄς ; τί λέξεις ; ὥς μʼ ἀπώλεσας , γύναι .
sections.translation: What do you mean ? What will you say ? Ah , my wife , you have ruined me .

sections.label: Section 780
sections.original: φεῦγʼ ὡς τάχιστα τῆσδʼ ἀπαλλαχθεὶς χθονός .
sections.translation: Escape from this land and flee as quickly as possible . The man who lives in this house will kill you .

sections.label: Section 782
sections.original: τί χρῆμα δράσας ἄξιον τῆς συμφορᾶς ;
sections.translation: What have I done to deserve such a fate ?

sections.label: Section 783
sections.original: ἥκεις ἄελπτος ἐμποδὼν ἐμοῖς γάμοις .
sections.translation: You have come unexpectedly to hinder my marriage .

sections.label: Section 784
sections.original: ἦ γὰρ γαμεῖν τις τἄμʼ ἐβουλήθη λέχη ;
sections.translation: What ! Does someone plan to marry my wife ?

sections.label: Section 785
sections.original: ὕβριν θʼ ὑβρίζειν εἰς ἔμʼ , ἣν ἔτλην ἐγώ .
sections.translation: And to act in violence against me , which I have endured .

sections.label: Section 786
sections.original: ἰδίᾳ σθένων τις ἢ τυραννεύων χθονός ;
sections.translation: Does he have private power , or is he the ruler of the country ?

sections.label: Section 787
sections.original: ὃς γῆς ἀνάσσει τῆσδε Πρωτέως γόνος .
sections.translation: He is the lord of this land , the son of Proteus .

sections.label: Section 788
sections.original: τόδʼ ἔστʼ ἐκεῖνʼ αἴνιγμʼ ὃ προσπόλου κλύω .
sections.translation: This is that riddle I heard from the servant .

sections.label: Section 789
sections.original: ποίοις ἐπιστὰς βαρβάροις πυλώμασιν ;
sections.translation: Which one of the barbarian ’ s gates were you standing beside ?

sections.label: Section 790
sections.original: τοῖσδʼ , ἔνθεν ὥσπερ πτωχὸς ἐξηλαυνόμην .
sections.translation: This one , from which I was being driven away like a beggar .

sections.label: Section 791
sections.original: οὔ που προσῄτεις βίοτον ; ὦ τάλαινʼ ἐγώ .
sections.translation: You were surely not begging for food , were you ? How unhappy I am !
